observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

strictly adhere to the state policy on DRRM by giving priority on the approval and implementation of planned PPAs to achieve the objectives/goals of the government of a disaster resilient environment; 

direct the MPDO, together with the Municipal Engineer, to ensure the prompt implementation and completion of DRRM projects under LGSF-DRRAP in accordance with the authorized timeline of the utilization of the fund; and

immediately submit request for extension of time on the utilization of NDRRM Fund and LGSF-FA and copy furnish the Audit Team of the request and result thereof for further validation.
observations.managementAction:
Only five out of 12 PPAs under the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund was implemented for CY 2022.



DRRM projects under LGSF-DRRAP was implemented in CY 2022



Request was submitted

Implemented projects under fund transfers totaling ₱6,750,395.50 were noted with various deficiencies, not in conformity to COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, thus gave doubts on the propriety, validity and regularity of transactions made.

Only five out of 12 PPAs under the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund was implemented.



No DRRM Implementing Plan was prepared



Few delays were still noted in the submission of DRRM liquidation reports | Justification was submitted

Implemented DRRM PPAs were in accordance with updated DRRM Implementing Plan/Detailed Budget Proposal

DV was already submitted to the Audit Team and subsequent liquidation was recorded

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the GFPS to maximize the utilization of GAD funds and monitor implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that no PPAs are left unimplemented at year-end; and

implement a defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on gender mainstreaming.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU was only able to implement 12 out of 28 PPAs comprising only 2.02 per cent of the total annual budget.



No monitoring and evaluation system was implemented

Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

conduct a complete inventory of all the supplies and materials inventory and submit a complete inventory report of all supplies inventoried to the audit team; 

direct the General Services Office to maintain complete supply ledger cards and stock cards and maintain a strong internal control on property management system for appropriate recording and ensure that all property is safeguarded and accounted for; and

direct the concerned management personnel on the distribution of relief goods and other supplies and materials for distribution to submit the necessary reports/records, RSMI and supporting documents such as distribution lists to accounting unit for proper accounting and recording in the books.
observations.managementAction:
No inventory report submitted to the Audit Team for validation | Stock cards are being maintained



Necessary reports/records and supporting documents were submitted to accounting office for recording

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the cleansing and updating of taxpayers’ records (RPTARs/RPTICs) to arrive at the correct/accurate List of Name of Taxpayers and the Amount Due and Collectible for the year with proper disclosure, if necessary, on the collectivity and/or other concern affecting the RPT and SEF Receivables accounts, to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and to correct the balance of the same accounts.

We recommended that Management:

direct the Municipal Health Officer to provide the Municipal Accountant with correct, accurate, and complete wall-to-wall inventory reports including data on expired vaccines, RIS, and other duly accomplished supporting documents as well as the cost of the vaccines and supplies by coordinating with the PHO and DOH, Region V to properly record and/or disclose in the books the inventory of all COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary immunization supplies in accordance with the cited COA circular and accounting policies under paragraph 96 and 97 of IPSAS 23;

instruct the Municipal Health Officer to properly record, monitor, verify, and update the details indicated in the stock cards, wall-to-wall inventory, and other reports/documents about the receipt and utilization of the COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary immunization supplies for proper monitoring and accurate reporting thereof, including the expired vaccines, subject for return to PHO and DOH, Region V; and

coordinate with DOH thru PHO as to the appropriate action to be taken on their remaining vaccines since there are fewer people now willing to be vaccinated and the vaccines are due for expiration.

We recommended that Management consider requesting for write-off of the dormant Receivable accounts totaling ₱916,866.59, subject to documentary and procedural requirements under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and supplemental guidelines under COA Circular No. 2022-001, in the event of subsequent settlement of accounts previously written off is made.

We recommended that Management:

direct the concerned implementing office of the unfinished DENR and DTI projects to immediately facilitate the process of implementation of the projects, otherwise, return the unutilized portion of the project to the source agencies;

pursue the appropriate legal action against the erring contractor of the resettlement project to facilitate its overdue liquidation report and return of unused funds, if any to NHA; and

direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the return of an unutilized portion of completed projects to their respective source agencies for utilization of the funds to other national government projects.

We recommended that Management:

incorporate the four criteria set by E.O. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82 in the existing travel and training policy of the LGU and encourage the LGU officials and employees to prudently and strictly observe the same; and

avail of alternatives to travel, such as transmittal of documents via registered mail or accredited courier, if possible, to minimize travel costs against the LGU funds and avoid the need to augment from other items under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) class.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to its responsibility in ensuring that the 20 percent LDF is well-planned, procurement-and-implementation- ready, promptly implemented, and completed on target time to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU’s intended beneficiaries.

We also recommended that the PR, PO, and IAR of goods/items be properly prepared to ensure that LGU arrives at a reasonable contract cost and receives delivered items at terms that are most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that Management: 

direct the Inventory Committee to conduct on a full-time basis and within a specific period of time, a complete annual physical count, including assignment of property number stickers to all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine condition and valuation, and then prepare the RPCPPE; 

designate personnel each from the accounting and property offices who shall maintain and update the PPE Ledger/Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records, as well as maintain updated ARE with complete and specific description and safekeeping of all pertinent documents relative to the acquisition of properties; and

instruct all the departments to fill out the ARE completely and correctly with necessary details such as brand name, color, model, applicable serial number, and other important information of the property.
observations.managementAction:
A physical count was conducted but discrepancies were noted in the RPCPPE submitted.

Specific personnel not yet designated from General Services Office

While majority of the ARE was filled out completely and correctly with necessary details, there are still some ARE with no proper description of the PPE item.

Reiterated in Part II of this report.

The accounting office reconciled their records with the RPCPPE

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

General Services Officer:	
prepare a Report on Local Road Network;

maintain local road inventory and road map; and

maintain property cards for all roads and its components. 

Municipal Accountant	: 
maintain subsidiary ledger cards for roads and road components for every road network; and

prepare lapsing schedule showing the computation of the depreciation of each component of the Road Network System account.

Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer 
provide the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant together with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require the concerned accountable officers to settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances and/or refund unused portion, otherwise, enforce applicable sanctions; 



stop the grant of additional cash advances to personnel with unliquidated cash advances; and

establish controls to ensure full and timely liquidation of cash advances, such as requiring enough copies including the accountable official/employees’ file copy, and complete documentary requirements not only upon liquidation, but even upon the granting thereof to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction:
₱624,519.73 remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2021 and documents for dormant accounts were yet to be retrieved



Considerations for emergency expenses and overlapping activities | Considers issuance of certification of unavailability of documents, which may be attached when request for write off is undertaken.



Stricter on this

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the assigned personnel to Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts to coordinate with these agencies for reconciliation of respective records;

also direct the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to immediately coordinate with GSIS to reconcile mentioned differences in records and timely updating the member information as well as the rates of remittance. AAO is the official representative of a government agency who does not only approve loans, but also serves as a partner of the GSIS in the timely submission of updated information of the agency and its employees; and

enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the prescribed guidelines and timelines in the remittance of employees-members’ contributions to avoid penalties thereto and more importantly, to secure employees in availing of these agencies’ programs and services by way of proper and timely remittances by the LGU-employer.
observations.managementAction:
Records were reconciled



With the exception of few employees, majority of the member information was timely updated.



Strict compliance with the guidelines

observations.recommendation:

strictly adhere/oblige to its responsibility in ensuring that the 20% MDF is promptly implemented and completed on target time to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU’s constituents/intended beneficiaries; and 

 follow-up the funding for the Construction of Slaughterhouse from the DA-NMIS for the materialization of the project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Inventory Committee to conduct on a full-time basis and within a specific period of time, a complete annual physical count, including assignment of property number stickers to all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine condition and valuation, and then prepare the RPCPPE;
designate personnel each from the accounting and property offices who shall maintain and update the PPE Ledger/Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records, as well as maintain updated ARE with complete and specific description and safekeeping of all pertinent documents relative to the acquisition of properties; and
instruct all the departments to fill out completely and correctly the ARE with necessary details such as brand name, color, model, applicable serial number, and other important information of the property.

We recommended that Management require the:

The Management and the Municipal Engineer recognized the challenge in establishing the detailed road components but promised to initiate compliance in CY 2022.

General Services Officer | prepare a Report on Local Road Network;
maintain local road inventory and road map; and
maintain property cards for all roads and its components.
Municipal Accountant | maintain subsidiary ledger cards for roads and road components for every road network; and
prepare lapsing schedule showing the computation of the depreciation of each component of the Road Network System account.
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer | provide the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant together with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management:

strictly adhere to the state policy on DRRM by giving priority on the approval and implementation of planned PPAs to achieve the objectives/goals of the government of a disaster resilient environment;
direct the MPDO, together with the Municipal Engineer, to ensure the prompt implementation and completion of DRRM projects under LGSF-DRRAP in accordance with the authorized timeline of the utilization of the fund; and
immediately submit request for extension of time on the utilization of NDRRM Fund and LGSF-FA and copy furnish the Audit Team of the request and result thereof for further validation.

We recommended that Management:

compute the prospective LP, in reference to complete service record of PHWs, consistent with the pertinent provisions of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2012;
recompute the LP granted during CY 2021, subject to adjustment and/or refund of concerned PHWs if found not in order; and
formally seek update from OPCCB through RD of DBM.

We recommended that Management: (a) direct the concerned implementing offices to immediately present the lacking documents to substantiate the post-audit remarks and hasten the liquidation of welfare goods worth ₱2,596,587.00; (b)  direct the Bids and Award Committee to only accept completely filled-out procurement templates, refrain from erasures to ensure the regularity and integrity of  these documents and carefully assess the eligibility of suppliers; and (c) prospectively ensure complete documentation of all disbursements charged against the LGU funds.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to speed up the return of unused fund from fund transfer of national government.

We recommended that Management:

require the concerned accountable officers to settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances and/or refund unused portion, otherwise, enforce applicable sanctions;
stop the grant of additional cash advances to personnel with unliquidated cash advances; and
establish controls to ensure full and timely liquidation of cash advances, such as requiring enough copies including the accountable official/employees’ file copy, and complete documentary requirements not only upon liquidation, but even upon the granting thereof to establish accountability.

We recommended that Management:

direct the assigned personnel to Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts to coordinate with these agencies for reconciliation of respective records;
also direct the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to immediately coordinate with GSIS to reconcile mentioned differences in records and timely updating the member information as well as the rates of remittance. AAO is the official representative of a government agency who does not only approve loans, but also serves as a partner of the GSIS in the timely submission of updated information of the agency and its employees; and
enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the prescribed guidelines and timelines in the remittance of employees-members’ contributions to avoid penalties thereto and more importantly, to secure employees in availing of these agencies’ programs and services by way of proper and timely remittances by the LGU-employer.

We recommended that Management: (a) strictly adhere/oblige to its responsibility in ensuring that the 20% MDF is promptly implemented and completed on target time to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU’s constituents/intended beneficiaries; and (b) follow-up the funding for the Construction of Slaughterhouse from the DA-NMIS for the materialization of the project.

We recommended that Management:

direct the GFPS to maximize the utilization of GAD funds and monitor implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that no PPAs are left unimplemented at year-end; and
implement a defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on gender mainstreaming.
